svg.text: SHUTTER HUB — hub baseboard layout · plan view (KiCad-style)
★ set OUT = 5.10 V with a meter, unloaded, BEFORE connecting anything
16-channel servo header — V+ · GND · SIG rows · native 0.1" servo plugs
shown displaced ↑ — mounts on M2.5×15 nylon standoffs overhanging the top board edge
fixes to CASE LID behind a
18 AWG point-to-point — servo current NEVER via breadboard ⚠
centre gap — strips do not cross
antenna overhangs the board edge — keep off copper
2 × 19-pin female headers, cols 2–20 · pins land in rows a & j
wire by pin LABEL — clone pin order varies · 30-pin DevKit: cols 2–16
ESP32 pin used by this build
WIRE SCHEDULE — solder BEFORE fitting the ESP32 sockets
W1 VIN (a,20) → +5.1 V top rail top
W2 GND (a,15) → GND top rail under
W4 GND (j,2) → GND bottom rail under
W5 GND (j,8) → GND bottom rail under
W6 GPIO21 (j,7) → J2·SDA servo-bus SDA under
W7 GPIO22 (j,4) → J2·SCL servo-bus SCL under
W8 GPIO25 (a,10) → J3·SDA sensor-bus SDA under
W9 GPIO26 (a,11) → J3·SCL sensor-bus SCL under
W10 3V3 rail → J2·1 and J3·1 under
W11 GND rail → J2·2 and J3·2 under
W12 GND tie — top ↔ bottom GND rails, right edge
W13 J1 pads → +5.1 V rail (red) / GND rail (blue)
• Servo current (2–3 A peak) never touches breadboard copper —
18 AWG runs XL4015 OUT → PCA9685 V+ terminal; bulk cap at V+.
• Breadboard carries logic only (~300 mA): J1 → top 5.1 V rail →
ESP32 VIN. The ESP32 3V3 pin feeds the bottom 3V3 rail → J2/J3,
so the whole I2C side runs at 3.3 V (no level shifting).
• Both breakout boards carry their own I2C pull-ups — add none.
• Rail checks first: ✂ cut any factory top↔bottom red-rail link
(they carry DIFFERENT voltages); ⌒ bridge mid-board rail splits.
• Default firmware pins shown: servo bus GPIO21/22 → PCA9685 0x40;
• Power-up: 5.10 V set unloaded → boot with no servos → I2C scan
finds 0x40 + 0x10 → add one MG90D on CH0 and re-test.
• J2·OE spare — PCA9685 output-enable, works left n/c.
ESP32 SHUTTER HUB — breadboard layout (plan view)
ESP32-D DevKitC-38 · PCA9685 @0x40 · VEML7700 @0x10 · default pins
